news.title: Threads finally brings messaging to the web
news.summary: By bringing messaging to the web, Threads is aligning its desktop experience more closely with competitors like X and Bluesky.

news.title: Airbnb co-founder taps Peter Arnell as first US chief brand architect
news.summary: Arnell joins Gebbia’s National Design Studio under Trump to unify UX across 27,000 federal sites and streamline how Americans use government services.

news.title: Pennsylvania sues Character.AI after a chatbot allegedly posed as a doctor
news.summary: According to Pennsylvania's filing, a Character.AI chatbot presented itself as a licensed psychiatrist during a state investigation, and also fabricated a serial number for its state medical license.

news.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 Instant, a new default model for ChatGPT
news.summary: The company said the model reduces hallucination in sensitive areas such as law, medicine, and finance, while maintaining the low latency of its predecessor.

news.title: PayPal says it’s ‘becoming a technology company again’ — that means AI
news.summary: PayPal is pitching an AI-led turnaround, tying automation and restructuring to $1.5 billion in savings as it cuts jobs and works to modernize its tech stack.

news.title: Threads is finally getting DMs on the web
news.summary: Nearly a year after rolling out Threads direct messaging to everyone on mobile, Meta is finally bringing the feature to the web. news.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
news.summary: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. news.title: OpenAI claims ChatGPT’s new default model hallucinates way less
news.summary: OpenAI's newest default model for ChatGPT might not make stuff up as much. Hallucinations have been an ongoing problem for AI models, but OpenAI says its new GPT-5.5 Instant model has "significant improvements in factuality across the board."
